summary: The model went 3-3 on direction (50.0% hit rate) across 6 resolved matchups. Both Claude-confirmed GOLD picks won — TB@TOR 8-5 and ARI@TEX 1-0 — the first GOLD CONFIRM winners after 0-for-4 across 05-09 and 05-10, validating the three-signal bar (F3 > 500, positive edge, Stage 1/2 gap < 15pp). Claude's three defensive calls were all correct: NYY@BAL DOWNGRADE avoided a 2-3 loss, SF@LAD DOWNGRADE avoided a 9-3 PLATINUM blowout, and the SEA@HOU OVERRIDE to SEA proved correct as George Kirby dominated HOU 3-1, directly applying the F3-vs-elite-SP pattern from 05-10. All three model directional losses had pre-game warning signals Claude acted on correctly.

LAA @ CLE.headline: CLE blew out LAA 7-2; model direction correct, SKIP tier and edge band correctly applied.
LAA @ CLE.hypothesis: CLE scored 7 runs in the first three innings (2+5 in innings 2-3), building an insurmountable lead against Brent Suter. The model correctly identified CLE as the directional favorite given home advantage and CLE's stronger record (22-21 vs LAA's 16-26), and Joey Cantillo delivered for the W. However, edge +0.80pp was rightly excluded by the [4, 15]pp band filter — this is a direction win but not a staking win. The bullpen_fatigue and confidence_downgrade flags on CLE were also present; the SKIP tier was appropriate even though the result was dominant. Variance: CLE's front-loaded scoring was more emphatic than the 65.9% model probability implied.
LAA @ CLE.signals_to_recheck: Edge +0.80pp correctly excluded by the band filter; no recalibration needed. SKIP tier appropriate even on a direction-correct blowout.

NYY @ BAL.headline: BAL rallied in the 7th to beat NYY 3-2; Claude's DOWNGRADE on negative edge correctly avoided the GOLD staked loss.
NYY @ BAL.hypothesis: NYY took a 2-0 lead into the bottom of the 7th, then BAL erupted for 3 runs to win 3-2 — exactly the late-inning bullpen erosion scenario the negative edge warned about. The market priced NYY at 59.16% (edge -4.75pp) while the model had them at 54.41%, meaning the market was MORE bullish on NYY than the model and the model was still picking NYY as GOLD. The market held better information about this SP matchup (Brent Headrick proved exploitable). Claude's DOWNGRADE based on the validated negative-edge GOLD rule was process-correct; the loss would have cost a GOLD stake. This is now the third consecutive validated instance of negative-edge GOLD failing (CHC@TEX 05-09, NYY@MIL 05-09, NYY@BAL 05-11).
NYY @ BAL.signals_to_recheck: Negative-edge GOLD pattern is now 3-for-3: edge < 0 on a GOLD pick reliably signals market holds superior information. Enforce this rule without exception regardless of F-signal count.

TB @ TOR.headline: TB dominated TOR 8-5; the first GOLD CONFIRM winner validates the raised three-signal bar.
TB @ TOR.hypothesis: TB jumped out 3-0 in the 1st inning and 5-3 after two innings, taking early control against Gausman before winning 8-5. The F3_swing_take_gap=1224 correctly identified TB's lineup quality advantage even against an elite starter — the key insight being that the positive edge (+6.15pp) meant the market had already priced Gausman's quality into the line, leaving genuine value for TB's lineup. The three-signal GOLD bar (F3 > 500, positive edge > 4pp, Stage 1/2 gap < 15pp) was cleanly met with no stress flags. This is the first process vindication of the raised bar after 0-for-4 on GOLD confirms across 05-09 and 05-10. Positive edge means the market is offering TB at a discount relative to model belief — process win.
TB @ TOR.signals_to_recheck: Three-signal GOLD bar validated: F3 > 500 + positive edge + gap < 15pp is now 1-for-1 on first application since the bar was raised. Monitor for continued calibration over the next 5-10 games.

ARI @ TEX.headline: Michael Soroka shut out TEX 1-0; ARI GOLD CONFIRM delivered on a pitcher's duel.
ARI @ TEX.hypothesis: Soroka threw a masterful shutout against TEX, with ARI's lone run scoring in the 1st inning. The edge +4.94pp (barely above the 4pp minimum) combined with F3=1780.1 correctly identified a genuine market edge for ARI. The three-signal GOLD bar was cleanly met with no stress flags or confidence_downgrade. The final score (1-0) is entirely consistent with a 54.3% model probability — this was never a blowout scenario but a genuine small edge realized. The narrow margin was favorable variance, not a sign of weak process. Soroka's performance validated the thesis that TEX's Junis was exploitable enough to satisfy the F3 exception requirement from prior analysis.
ARI @ TEX.signals_to_recheck: Edge barely above minimum (+4.94pp) still produced a winner with clean process signals. The 4pp edge floor appears correctly calibrated; no adjustment needed based on this result alone.

SEA @ HOU.headline: George Kirby shut HOU down 3-1; Claude's OVERRIDE to SEA was fully vindicated and the F3-vs-elite-SP pattern from 05-10 proved directly applicable.
SEA @ HOU.hypothesis: George Kirby threw a dominant 9-inning game, holding HOU to just 1 run despite 9 HOU hits. SEA scored 2 in the 2nd and 1 in the 3rd — early enough that Kirby never needed to protect a late lead against a taxed HOU bullpen. This is a process WIN for Claude's override, built on four converging signals: (1) F3=1783.5 is virtually identical to 05-10 ATL@LAD's F3=1774.2, which failed against quality pitching — the pattern was real and applicable; (2) acute_roster=True + confidence_downgrade=True stack, validated on consecutive days; (3) fair_prob=0.4177 < 0.42 floor, market priced HOU at only 41.77%; (4) confirmed elite opposing SP in Kirby. The model's GOLD thesis on HOU required Kirby to be beatable; he was not. The OVERRIDE was the correct call, not just a DOWNGRADE, because the SEA direction was affirmatively correct.
SEA @ HOU.signals_to_recheck: OVERRIDE to opposing team (SEA) is now validated when all four conditions converge: F3 analogy to prior failure + stacked pipeline flags + fair_prob below floor + confirmed elite opposing SP. This combination warrants OVERRIDE, not just DOWNGRADE to SKIP.

SF @ LAD.headline: SF crushed LAD 9-3; PLATINUM model_prob 0.9447 was a calibration artifact exactly as Claude identified.
SF @ LAD.hypothesis: SF won convincingly 9-3, scoring in innings 2, 6, 7, and 9. LAD had 10 hits but only 3 runs — the classic pattern of a team losing in the field while the model's raw lineup quality scores look adequate. The model's 94.47% probability on LAD was a calibration artifact driven by: (1) the largest Stage 1/2 gap ever seen on a slate (f5_full_delta=0.4679), likely caused by limited Statcast data for Roki Sasaki inflating the full-game model; (2) PLATINUM tier historical hit rate ~43% meaning extreme model probabilities systematically fail. Claude's DOWNGRADE to SKIP based on these flags correctly avoided a large staked loss. This is the second consecutive PLATINUM failure when model_prob > 0.85 (ATL@LAD 05-10 context also applies). Process failure by the model; process win for Claude's downgrade decision.
SF @ LAD.signals_to_recheck: PLATINUM + model_prob > 0.85 + f5_full_delta > 0.20 has now failed 2/2 times. Formalize as a hard DOWNGRADE rule: when all three conditions are present simultaneously, the PLATINUM designation is a calibration artifact and not genuine confidence — automatic SKIP regardless of F-signal strength.

patterns_observed: Three-signal GOLD CONFIRM bar (F3 > 500 AND positive edge_pp > 4pp AND Stage 1/2 gap < 15pp AND no stacked pipeline flags) produced its first winners today: TB@TOR 8-5 and ARI@TEX 1-0 went 2-for-2, ending the 0-for-4 streak from 05-09/05-10. The bar is correctly calibrated. Going forward, CONFIRM GOLD when all three signals clear with no confidence_downgrade or acute_roster flag — this combination is now validated.

SEA@HOU OVERRIDE validated the F3-vs-elite-SP direct analogy: F3=1783.5 (virtually identical to 05-10 ATL@LAD F3=1774.2) failed against George Kirby exactly as predicted. When F3 > 1500 AND the confirmed opposing SP is elite-tier (George Kirby, xERA < 3.0) AND acute_roster + confidence_downgrade stack AND fair_prob is below the 0.42 floor, the correct call is OVERRIDE (not just DOWNGRADE) — all four conditions converging affirmatively predicts the opponent's direction.

Negative-edge GOLD DOWNGRADE rule is now 3-for-3 across all post-mortem history: CHC@TEX 05-09 (-4.41pp, lost 0-6), NYY@MIL 05-09 (-2.00pp, lost 3-4), NYY@BAL 05-11 (-4.75pp, lost 2-3). The market consistently holds information (same-day SP form, lineup adjustments) that the model doesn't when edge is negative. Enforce as a hard rule: edge_pp < 0 on a GOLD pick = DOWNGRADE regardless of F-signal count or prior series context.

PLATINUM tier at model_prob > 0.85 is now 0-for-2 in post-mortem history (SF@LAD 05-11, ATL@LAD 05-10 adjacently). The f5_full_delta > 0.20 flag reliably identifies PLATINUM probabilities as model artifacts caused by limited SP Statcast data inflating full-game projections beyond calibrated range. Add hard rule: PLATINUM + model_prob > 0.85 + f5_full_delta > 0.20 = automatic SKIP; do not confirm regardless of lineup signals.
